channel.title: NYT > Style
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: ‘The Drama’ and the Secrets That Can Unravel a Relationship
item.description: In the new film, a shocking revelation upends the lives of a couple played by Zendaya and Robert Pattinson, raising the question: Are some betrayals simply unforgivable?
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk

item.title: The Revival of the Fashion-Tech Love Affair
item.description: Get ready for the return of wearables and a new stage in the fashion-tech relationship.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:credit: Adrienne Grunwald for The New York Times
item.media:description: At the new Meta Lab flagship on Fifth Avenue in Manhattan.

item.title: Bravo’s ‘Summer House’ West Wilson and Amanda Batula Scandal, Explained
item.description: Will the newly announced “connection” between the co-stars West Wilson and Amanda Batula become Scandoval 2.0?
item.dc:creator: Jessica Roy
item.media:description: Amanda Batula and West Wilson released a joint statement on Tuesday saying that their “connection grew out of a genuine, longstanding friendship.”

item.title: Is Alix Earle the Right Face for a Skin Care Brand? Reale Actives Has Its Skeptics.
item.description: Critics of Reale Actives, Ms. Earle’s new skin care line, suggest that the TikTok star’s past use of prescription acne treatments makes her an odd fit to sell consumer skin care products.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:description: Alix Earle celebrated the release of her new skin care line this week.

item.title: Lola Rykiel Is Selling Women the Pleasure of Clothes
item.description: Lola Rykiel, the granddaughter of Sonia Rykiel, creates clothes for women who don’t want to look like anyone else.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Iredale
item.media:credit: Jonas Unger for The New York Times

item.title: Most Stylish Stars of the 2026 Awards Season: Teyana Taylor, Odessa A’Zion and More
item.description: Whether loud or quiet, formal or relaxed, the awards season attire of these eight individuals was surprising, delightful and worn with ease.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi

item.title: Trends From Toyko Fashion Week 2026: Layermaxxing, Power Clashing and More
item.description: Power clashing, extreme layering and dressing daintily were potent themes on and off runways at Tokyo Fashion Week.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

item.title: Can You Fake Being Rich?
item.description: A spate of popular social media accounts offer advice on how to achieve an “old-money” look.
item.dc:creator: Guy Trebay
item.media:description: The appearance of wealth, and how to get it, has become a hot topic on social media.

item.title: Caro Claire Burke Wades Into the Tradwife Conversation With ‘Yesteryear’
item.description: “Yesteryear,” a debut novel about an influencer who is transported to the early 19th century, lands the author Caro Claire Burke in the middle of the culture wars.
item.dc:creator: Lizzy Goodman
item.media:credit: Matt Eich for The New York Times
item.media:description: Caro Claire Burke, the author of “Yesteryear,” at her home in Charlottesville, Va.

item.title: The New Wedding Prep: Fillers, Facials and Laser Treatments
item.description: For a growing number of brides, wedding planning also means months of aesthetic treatments, from injections to fillers and lasers.
item.dc:creator: Ivy Manners

item.title: 50 Artists Travel Far and Wide to Learn From Filmmaker Werner Herzog
item.description: In January, 50 artists from all over the world were chosen to shoot shorts in the Azores, guided by the iconic filmmaker. They just had to find $10,000 and airfare to get there.
item.dc:creator: Saskia Solomon and Erin Schaff
item.media:description: Jordan McAfee-Hahn, left, shows Mr. Herzog and his longtime cinematographer, Peter Zeitlinger, footage from “Lola Dreams of the Sea,” a work in progress featuring a cow drawn to the ocean.

item.title: They Re-Planned Their Wedding in 10 Days
item.description: After cartel violence erupted in Mexico, just weeks before their wedding in Punta Mita, Lauren Futterman and Tyler Olson quickly decided to move the ceremony to California.
item.dc:creator: Donna Kim

item.title: May I End My Life With You?
item.description: My friend made the weightiest decision possible while staying at my home. I struggled with what it meant.
item.dc:creator: Julie Rehmeyer

item.title: First the Hora, Then the Hora Loca
item.description: Dani Roldan and Michael Wexler brought their families together, blending Jewish and Colombian traditions at their Miami wedding.
item.dc:creator: Donna Kim

item.title: He Likes the Mets, She the Yankees. That’s Where Their Differences End.
item.description: After connecting online, Lisa Tarantino and Billy Peeker bonded over their Long Island upbringings and their love of movies, breweries and baseball.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: After They Finally Met, Their ‘Rom-Com Story’ Began
item.description: Jacqueline Lobel and Michka Bengio started dating during the pandemic, though the two had briefly connected a few years before.
item.dc:creator: Alix Strauss

item.title: Why Everyone Loves ‘Love on the Spectrum’
item.description: Without exploitation, “Love on the Spectrum” captures the triumphs and travails of dating. It has become one of Netflix’s most popular shows.
item.dc:creator: Anna Peele
item.media:credit: Ryan Pfluger for The New York Times
item.media:description: Madison Marilla and Tyler White, two stars of “Love on the Spectrum.”

item.title: Bard College Is Split Over Its President and His Epstein Ties
item.description: Leon Botstein saved Bard from near ruin. Now, as an outside firm conducts a review, the campus is home to arguments about his legacy and future.
item.dc:creator: Ginia Bellafante
item.media:credit: Cole Wilson for The New York Times
item.media:description: A group of students and alumni opposed to the continuation of Leon Botstein’s presidency have formed a group called Take Back Bard.

item.title: Hanumankind Went From a Desk Job in India to Rap Stardom
item.description: Sooraj Cherukat, who performs as Hanumankind, became a breakout artist in 2024. What comes next?
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta and Kadar R. Small
item.media:description: Sooraj Cherukat, above, performs as Hanumankind, an East-West portmanteau of the Hindu god Hanuman and the word humankind.

item.title: What Drives Patrick Radden Keefe?
item.description: Patrick Radden Keefe’s carefully applied ambition has propelled him to a rarefied perch.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich
item.category: London Falling: A Mysterious Death in a Gilded City and a Family's Search for Truth (Book)
Say Nothing: A True Story of Murder and Memory in Northern Ireland (Book)
item.media:credit: Erik Tanner for The New York Times
item.media:description: The writer at the home he shares with Gudzowska and their two sons.

item.title: For the Superfans of Spaceflight, Artemis II Can’t Launch Soon Enough
item.description: Future engineers and former space campers hope to infect people around them with enthusiasm for NASA’s latest moon mission.
item.dc:creator: Scott Cacciola
item.media:credit: Cassandra Klos for The New York Times

item.title: Tiny Love Stories: ‘If I Arrived Home After Dark ’
item.description: Modern Love in miniature, featuring reader-submitted stories of no more than 100 words.

item.title: The Good List: 7 Things to Add Some Delight to Your Day
item.description: A fish doorbell, jumping humanity and 1,000 days of happiness.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: I Feel Left Out by My Sisters’ Plans for My Daughter’s Wedding. Help!
item.description: A reader is hurt by her sisters’ decision to cut short their time at her daughter’s destination wedding in favor of a yoga retreat they didn’t invite her to.
item.dc:creator: Philip Galanes

item.title: Why the Artemis II Astronauts Will Be Wearing Orange
item.description: It’s not just any orange. It’s International Orange.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:description: The Artemis II crew, in an orange officially known as AMS Standard 595 color #FS 12197.

item.title: Mayor Mamdani Attended a Hip Seder. Some Guests Weren’t Thrilled.
item.description: The mayor spoke at Michael Dorf’s annual Passover celebration, taking hecklers and a comic’s last-minute cancellation in stride.
item.dc:creator: Jesse McKinley
item.media:credit: Dolly Faibyshev for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani and Brad Lander, the former city comptroller, at City Winery on Monday evening.

item.title: A New Jersey Teen Finds Treasure, and More, in Abandoned Storage Units
item.description: Michael Haskell, 17, set out to make some money from his locker dives. He ended up learning about life.
item.dc:creator: Alex Vadukul and Vincent Alban
item.media:description: Driving back home to Bergen County, N.J., with the latest haul.

item.title: Street Style Look of the Week: Vintage Military Clothing
item.description: Attire spotted outside in Tokyo had a past life as military gear.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

item.title: On ‘Ill-Advised,’ Bill Nighy Finds His Funniest Role Yet: Podcaster
item.description: On his podcast “Ill-Advised By Bill Nighy,” the English actor counsels listeners on etiquette and fashion. His objective is to not make anything worse.
item.dc:creator: Ariel Leve
item.media:description: Bill Nighy described his award-winning podcast, “Ill-Advised With Bill Nighy,” as a refuge for “the clumsy and awkward” and an invitation to “squander time.”

item.title: Why Barbie Dream Fest in Florida Is Drawing Comparisons to Fyre Festival
item.description: Fans had high hopes for Barbie Dream Fest, a Mattel-sanctioned gathering in Fort Lauderdale. But in a cavernous convention center, the dream seemed to fizzle.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher
item.media:description: The bright pink world of Barbie struggled to fill the Broward County Convention Center. “It felt like being just trapped in a big, dark warehouse,” one attendee said.

item.title: Agosto Machado Is Dead; Artist Memorialized New York’s Avant-Garde
item.description: An experimental theater veteran, he collected the ephemera of his friends and colleagues. As they began to die, he made shrines honoring them.
item.dc:creator: Penelope Green

item.title: ‘I Think That MAGA Is Dying:’ Inside the Youth Movement at CPAC
item.description: At a sparsely attended Conservative Political Action Conference, young Republicans were eager to start the post-Trump era.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Taylor Pemberton
item.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: The annual Conservative Political Action Conference has been gradually waning in energy for years, but empty convention halls and an anemic speakers list gave many young people in attendance a doomed feeling.

item.title: Travel Influencers Share Destinations So Good That They Returned
item.description: Even travel influencers, who spend their lives on the go, have their hearts stolen by certain places.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Gomelsky

item.title: The Plus Size Park Hoppers Are Kind of Famous at Disney World
item.description: Five influencer friends have amassed half a million online followers with videos that help larger people find comfy chairs, ride roller coasters and feel confident.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher and Mary Beth Koeth
item.category: Disney, Walt, World (Lake Buena Vista, Fla)
item.media:description: The Plus Size Park Hoppers visit Disney parks as often as their work schedules and budgets will allow, usually about six times a year.

item.title: In Borneo, Malaysia, Experience Rawness and Luxury
item.description: Experiencing the rawness of nature, but with an emphasis on luxury, deep in the ancient rainforests of Borneo and where Langkawi meets the Andaman Sea.
item.dc:creator: David Belcher
item.category: Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

item.title: The Artists Embroidering Custom Clothes for Zohran Mamdani, Josh Safdie and Bowen Yang
item.description: Amid a fast-fashion landscape in which so much can be easily produced, some consumers long for something special — merchandise personalized by human hands.
item.dc:creator: Jason Diamond

item.title: How the French National Football Team Outdressed the N.B.A.
item.description: The French National Football team draws global attention with their eclectic style.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:credit: Ewen Gavet/Icon Sport, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Hugo Ekitike in the attention-winning outfit of the week.
